author: Dr. Vivian Chen (Dermatology) / Dr. Sarah Lo (Dermatopathology)
content:
DERMATOLOGY CONSULT — HOSPITAL DAY 6, WITH SKIN BIOPSY PATHOLOGY
Date/Time: 3/20/2027, 3:30 PM
Patient: Sandoval, Maria Isabel (MRN: MRN-90215734)
Referring service: Internal Medicine / Rheumatology
Interpreter: Spanish phone interpreter present.

REASON FOR CONSULT:
Evaluation of scaling dermatosis. Re-biopsy from active 'flaky paint' lesion on abdomen requested.

BRIEF HX:
23-year-old woman with established active SLE, severe weight loss, and diffuse scaling dermatosis progressive over past 2-3 months. Prior skin biopsy 2/10/2027 (during AMA admission) from left elbow read as 'interface dermatitis with vacuolar changes... features that could be seen in nutritional dermatosis.'

DERMATOLOGIC EXAMINATION (bedside, full skin survey):
Vitals: stable. Patient cachectic, anasarcic.

Hair: Diffuse non-scarring scalp alopecia, prominent. On close inspection of multiple long strands, alternating bands of normal dark-brown pigmentation and lighter (almost reddish-tan) pigmentation along single hair shafts. Hair brittle, tugs easily.

Nails: Pale nail beds. Multiple transverse depressions on fingernails, particularly the thumb, index, and middle. No nail pitting. No periungual erythema. No nail-fold capillary changes visible to gross inspection.

Face: Slight facial fullness from corticosteroid effect overlapping with cachexia. No frank malar rash today. NO heliotrope (no purplish eyelid discoloration). Mild bilateral periorbital edema. Lips dry, mildly cracked. Tongue: small (~3 mm) shallow ulcer; no glossitis.

Trunk and extremities:
- DIFFUSE, well-demarcated to ill-demarcated, dry, pinkish-brown to hyperpigmented, fine to coarsely scaling plaques covering the V-area of chest, upper back, abdomen, and bilateral upper and lower extremities.
- On abdomen and thighs: irregularly shaped, well-demarcated patches of darker, scaly skin alternating with normal-appearing skin, with overlying fine desquamation. In one area of the right lateral abdomen, ~3 × 5 cm patch where the skin appears to be lifting off in coarse flakes.
- Over dorsum of MCPs and PIPs of both hands: pinker, slightly more raised patches with mild scale.
- On extensor surfaces of elbows and knees: dry, scaly, hyperpigmented plaques. The left elbow site of the prior 2/10 biopsy is healing with visible scar tissue; surrounding skin matches the broader dermatosis pattern.
- No definite discoid lupus lesions (no atrophic scarring discoid plaques).
- No frank vasculitic lesions (no purpura, no ulcerations).
- No livedo reticularis.
- No urticarial vasculitis.
- No Raynaud-related digital changes.
- No additional mucous membrane involvement beyond the tongue ulcer.

Genitalia/perineum (with chaperone, brief inspection): No genital ulcers. Diffuse dryness and scaling consistent with overall pattern.

BIOPSY:
Fresh punch biopsy (4 mm) from the right lateral abdomen patch under local 1% lidocaine. Single suture closure. Specimen sent to dermatopathology with request for PAS (fungal), GMS (fungal), Congo Red (amyloid), immunofluorescence (lupus band test).

---

DERMATOPATHOLOGY REPORT — finalized 3/22/2027:

SPECIMEN: Punch biopsy, right lateral abdomen, 3/20/2027.

MICROSCOPIC EXAMINATION:

EPIDERMIS:
- Mild flattening with broad attenuation in some areas.
- Hyperkeratosis with overlying parakeratosis in pockets.
- Focal areas of intraepidermal pallor with clear cytoplasmic vacuolation of keratinocytes in the upper spinous and granular layers.
- Mild basal vacuolar changes at the dermal-epidermal junction in some sections.
- No striking acantholysis.
- No suprabasilar cleft.
- Scattered apoptotic keratinocytes.

DERMIS:
- Sparse to moderate superficial perivascular lymphocytic infiltrate.
- A few histiocytes scattered in the upper dermis.
- Mild solar elastosis given photoexposed location.
- Dermal mucin: NOT INCREASED on alcian blue.
- No granulomas. No vasculitis. No frank lymphocyte exocytosis.

DERMAL-EPIDERMAL JUNCTION:
- Vacuolar changes present focally but NOT as pronounced as classic SLE interface dermatitis.
- No subepidermal blister formation.

IMMUNOFLUORESCENCE (lupus band test):
- IgG: focal, faint, granular deposition at the DEJ.
- IgM: focal granular deposition.
- IgA: negative.
- C3: focal granular deposition.
- Pattern is overall WEAKLY POSITIVE in a non-classic distribution.

SPECIAL STAINS:
- PAS: no fungal organisms.
- GMS: no fungal organisms.
- Congo Red: no amyloid.

DERMATOPATHOLOGIC DIAGNOSIS:
Interface/vacuolar dermatitis with epidermal cytoplasmic vacuolation of upper-layer keratinocytes. Mild lupus band test (weakly positive). Absent dermal mucin. No infection, no autoimmune blistering disease, no granuloma, no amyloidosis. Differential supports overlapping features: interface dermatitis consistent with CTD on background of changes also seen in deficiency/metabolic dermatoses. Clinical correlation recommended.

DERM RECOMMENDATIONS:
1. Continue baseline SLE-directed therapy.
2. Topical skin care: petroleum-based emollients, gentle cleansing, sun protection.
3. Avoid topical corticosteroids over large areas given systemic immunosuppression.
4. Re-evaluate at 1 month outpatient.

Recommendations communicated to rheumatology (Dr. Park) and nutrition (Eun-Ji Lee, RD) at 4 PM. Patient and mother updated through interpreter.
attestation:
Electronically signed by:
Dr. Vivian Chen, MD (Dermatology, Consultant Attending) — procedure and clinical impression
Dr. Sarah Lo, MD (Dermatopathology, Pathologist of Record) — histopathologic interpretation
Date: 3/20/2027 (consult); 3/22/2027 (pathology finalized)
